research.product: Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — cancelWorkRelationship worker action
research.interface: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM (farws), resource version 11.13.18.05, REST framework version 4

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Cancel a work relationship
official_sources.used_for: exact action path /workers/{workersUniqID}/child/workRelationships/{PeriodOfServiceId}/action/cancelWorkRelationship; Content-Type application/vnd.oracle.adf.action+json with REST-Framework-Version 4; actionCode/reasonCode request parameters; 200 response with application/vnd.oracle.adf.actionresult+json body {"returnValue":"true"}

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Get all worker work relationships
official_sources.used_for: workRelationships child collection path; q filtering on LegalEmployerName/WorkerType; PeriodOfServiceId as the relationship identifier; guidance that workersUniqID must be obtained by querying the workers collection, never generated by the client

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Status Codes
official_sources.used_for: documented status semantics driving the classifier and workflow: 200 OK, 201 Created, 202 Accepted (request accepted, processing not complete), 204 No Content, 4xx client errors (400/401/403/404/409/412/428), 5xx server errors

official_sources.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — Setting the REST Framework Version
official_sources.used_for: REST-Framework-Version header; version-4-and-above structured error document application/vnd.oracle.adf.error+json with title, status (string), o:errorDetails[].detail/o:errorCode/o:errorPath

research.language_rationale: The HR workflow automation this joins is a .NET service; Oracle publishes no official .NET SDK for Fusion HCM REST and documents the surface as plain REST with vendor media types, so HttpClient with System.Text.Json against the documented endpoints is the credible integration path and keeps the seed hermetic.

verified_facts: cancelWorkRelationship is a documented custom action: POST /workers/{workersUniqID}/child/workRelationships/{PeriodOfServiceId}/action/cancelWorkRelationship.
The action request media type is application/vnd.oracle.adf.action+json and the documented parameters are actionCode and reasonCode (valid values from actionsLOV/actionReasonsLOV).
The documented success reply is 200 with application/vnd.oracle.adf.actionresult+json and a returnValue field; the docs' example response serializes it as the string "true".
workersUniqID is a hash key that clients must obtain from the workers collection (e.g. from an item's self link), never construct.

The framework documents 202 Accepted as 'accepted for processing, processing has not completed' — the asynchronous variant a client must be prepared to follow up on; this fixture pins the tenant's Location-poll-until-404 follow-up.
Canceling a work relationship removes it, so a poll of the relationship's own URL answering 404 marks completion.
With framework version 4+, failures answer application/vnd.oracle.adf.error+json with title, status (string), and an ordered o:errorDetails array of detail/o:errorCode/o:errorPath entries.
